title: gonystylus consanguineus
intro: Gonystylus consanguineus, also known as the "Malaysian ironwood," is a species of plant that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, and has also been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Gonystylus consanguineus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries in various parts of the world.
